enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Choose the correct technical word related to modern technology from the options provided.

questions.texte: To save a file from the internet onto your computer or phone, you need to ______ it.
When you move through different websites or pages on the internet, you are ______.
A smartphone, a tablet, and a smartwatch are all examples of modern technological ______.
Before you can send an email, you need to ______ to your email account using a username and password.
If you want to remove a file from your computer completely, you should ______ it.

enonce.texte: Read the following real-life situations. Use the vocabulary from Lesson 36 (download, upload, browse, click, device, gadget, log in, delete, install, update) to complete the tasks.

questions.texte: Your younger brother wants to listen to music on his new phone, but he has no internet connection. What two actions should he do first?
questions.reponse: He should first connect to Wi-Fi or mobile data, then download the songs.

questions.texte: You receive an email that looks suspicious (a scam). What are three safe actions to take?
questions.reponse: Do not click on any links, do not download any attachments, and delete the email immediately.

questions.texte: Your teacher asks you to submit a homework document online. List the correct steps in order.
questions.reponse: 1. Log in to the class platform. 2. Browse to find the homework file on my computer. 3. Click on 'submit' or 'upload' to send the document.

questions.texte: A friend in your village says, 'My phone is very slow.' Give two pieces of advice using technical vocabulary.
questions.reponse: You should delete old files or apps you don't use, and install the latest system update if available.

questions.texte: Your aunt has a new tablet. She is afraid of pressing the wrong thing. How would you explain the word 'browse' to her in simple terms?
questions.reponse: Browsing means looking at different pages or websites on the internet without a specific goal, just like flipping through a magazine to see what's interesting.

intitule: Problem-Solving: Technology for Community Development (Competence Avancée)

enonce.texte:
Read the scenario below from a small town in Cameroon and answer the questions that follow.

Scenario: In the town of Mbalmayo, the local library has three old computers, but they are very slow and often crash. Many students want to use them for research. The mayor has a small budget. You are a youth advisor.

questions.texte: Identify the main problem using two technical terms.
questions.reponse: The main problem is that the devices are outdated and need an update to their operating system or hardware.

questions.texte: Propose two low-cost solutions that do not involve buying brand new computers.
questions.reponse: 1. Install a free, lightweight operating system (like Linux) that runs faster on old devices. 2. Use the budget to update the RAM (memory) or replace hard drives with cheaper SSDs.

questions.texte: Write a short message (two sentences) to the mayor explaining why investing in these devices will help students, using the word 'browse' and 'download'.
questions.reponse: Upgrading the library's devices will allow students to browse academic websites for research. They will also be able to download important educational materials for their studies.
